# murmur3

```go
import "."
```

Package murmur3 implements Austin Appleby's non\-cryptographic MurmurHash3.

```
Reference implementation:
   http://code.google.com/p/smhasher/wiki/MurmurHash3

History, characteristics and (legacy) perfs:
   https://sites.google.com/site/murmurhash/
   https://sites.google.com/site/murmurhash/statistics
```

<a name="Sum128"></a>

## func [Sum128](<https://github.com/spaolacci/murmur3/blob/master/murmur128.go#L185>)

```go
func Sum128(data []byte) (h1 uint64, h2 uint64)
```

Sum128 returns the MurmurHash3 sum of data. It is equivalent to the following sequence \(without the extra burden and the extra allocation\):

```
hasher := New128()
hasher.Write(data)
return hasher.Sum128()
```

<a name="Sum128WithSeed"></a>

## func [Sum128WithSeed](<https://github.com/spaolacci/murmur3/blob/master/murmur128.go#L192>)

```go
func Sum128WithSeed(data []byte, seed uint32) (h1 uint64, h2 uint64)
```

Sum128WithSeed returns the MurmurHash3 sum of data. It is equivalent to the following sequence \(without the extra burden and the extra allocation\):

```
hasher := New128WithSeed(seed)
hasher.Write(data)
return hasher.Sum128()
```

return hasher.Sum32()

<a name="Hash128"></a>

## type [Hash128](<https://github.com/spaolacci/murmur3/blob/master/murmur128.go#L24-L27>)

Hash128 represents a 128\-bit hasher Hack: the standard api doesn't define any Hash128 interface.

```go
type Hash128 interface {
    hash.Hash
    Sum128() (uint64, uint64)
}
```
